fact: April 8 is exactly 98 days into the year - that's like counting to 98 every single morning since January 1st!
In the Northern Hemisphere, earthworms are going absolutely crazy right now, wiggling up to the surface after spring rains and making birds super happy for easy snacks.
Cherry blossom trees in Japan are either blooming or just finishing their pink explosion around this time, creating literal pink snow when the petals fall.
The sun is getting stronger every day now - in many northern places, you can actually feel the warmth on your face for the first time since winter ended.
Frogs in ponds across North America are starting their loud, croaky concerts at night, with some making sounds so loud they can be heard a mile away!
April 8 falls during Aries season, which runs from March 21 to April 19 in the zodiac calendar.
In the Southern Hemisphere, leaves are turning brilliant orange and red right now as autumn kicks in - it's like nature's own fireworks show.
Dandelions are typically popping up everywhere in northern lawns around this time, and each fluffy seed head contains dozens of seeds ready to fly away on the next breeze.
The days are getting about 2-3 minutes longer each day in northern places, which means bedtime stays bright longer and parents have to work harder to convince kids it's actually nighttime.
Migrating birds are often flying overhead in huge V-shaped flocks in North America, with some geese traveling over 3,000 miles and honking the entire way like they're complaining about the long trip.
In many northern places, this is prime time for finding four-leaf clovers since the grass is growing fast but hasn't been mowed much yet - your luck-hunting odds are actually better right now.

The moon phase around April 8 changes every year, but when it's a new moon near this date, the night sky becomes perfect for seeing shooting stars and planets without any moonlight getting in the way.
